1.gl: the face (as the part that turns); used in a great variety of applications (literally and figuratively); also (with prepositional prefix) as a preposition (before, etc.)
1.kj: [phrase] accept, a-(be-) fore(-time), against, anger, [idiom] as (long as), at, [phrase] battle, [phrase] because (of), [phrase] beseech, countenance, edge, [phrase] employ, endure
1.dv: plural (but always as singular) of an unused noun פָּנֶה; from H6437 (פָּנָה));

1.gl: properly, to glitter from afar, i.e. to be eminent (as a superintendent, especially of the Temple services and its music); to be permanent
1.kj: excel, chief musician (singer), oversee(-r), set forward.
1.dv: a primitive root;  also as denominative from H5331 (נֶצַח)

1.gl: to bolt, i.e. figuratively, to flee suddenly
1.kj: chase (away); drive away, fain, flee (away), put to flight, make haste, reach, run away, shoot.

1.gl: 'Thou must not destroy'; probably the opening words to a popular song
Shaul, the name of an Edomite and two Israelites
David, the youngest son of Jesse
1.dv: rarely (fully); דָּוִיד; from the same as H1730 (דּוֹד); loving;

2.gl: to cross over; used very widely of any transition (literal or figurative; transitive, intransitive, intensive, causative); specifically, to cover (in copulation)

2.gl: gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a superlative
2.kj: angels, [idiom] exceeding, God (gods) (-dess, -ly), [idiom] (very) great, judges, [idiom] mighty.

3.kj: angels, [idiom] exceeding, God (gods) (-dess, -ly), [idiom] (very) great, judges, [idiom] mighty.

to end (in the sense of completion or failure)
